core_concept: My Geography: Town, State, and Country
facts: A town is a smaller place than a city; a city is a large town, so a town can grow into a city if it gets big enough.
The United States is made up of 50 states, not just one big country; each state has its own name, like Texas or Ohio.
I live in a town inside a state, and that state is part of the United States; my town is not the whole country because there are many other towns and states.
misconceptions: A child may think the whole United States is just one big place called 'America' with no separate states, so they say 'I live in America' instead of 'I live in [state].'
